title: Divergent urban and rural environmental drivers of foodborne norovirus and its dissociation from the paediatric disease burden: a Bayesian spatial analysis across South Korean districts, 2020–2024 — analysis code
description: Reproducible R (R-INLA) analysis code for a district-level Bayesian negative-binomial Besag–York–Mollié spatial analysis of foodborne norovirus across South Korean districts, 2020–2024 (urban/rural stratified; paediatric dissociation test; BYM2, neighbourhood-graph and prior robustness; case-versus-outbreak sensitivity). Manuscript submitted to Science of the Total Environment. Raw data are aggregated official statistics with no personal identifiers and are not redistributed.

creators.name: Kim, Seongdae
creators.affiliation: Hongik University, Seoul, Republic of Korea
